examples.title: Node Structure and Basic Operations in C
examples.description: Complete implementation of singly linked list with insert, delete, and display operations
examples.explanation: This C program implements singly linked list with three main operations: insertAtBeginning (O(1)), insertAtEnd (O(n)), and deleteNode (O(n) for search + O(1) for deletion). The struct Node defines the self-referential structure with data and next pointer. Memory is allocated using malloc and freed using free.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for insertAtEnd and delete, O(1) for insertAtBeginning
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary, O(n) total for n nodes

examples.title: Singly Linked List in Python
examples.description: Python implementation with Node class and LinkedList class methods
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses classes to encapsulate the linked list. Key methods: insert_at_beginning (O(1)), insert_at_end (O(n)), delete (O(n)), reverse (O(n)), and find_middle using slow-fast pointer technique (O(n)). Python's garbage collection handles memory automatically.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for most operations except insert_at_beginning which is O(1)

examples.title: Singly Linked List in Java
examples.description: Java implementation with generic type and comprehensive methods
examples.explanation: This Java implementation uses generics (T) to make the list type-safe for any data type. It includes an inner static Node class, methods for insertion at both ends, deletion, reversal, and cycle detection using Floyd's algorithm. The generic implementation allows storing integers, strings, or any object type.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for most operations, O(1) for insertFirst
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space for all operations

examples.title: Algorithm: Reversing a Singly Linked List
examples.description: Iterative algorithm with step-by-step logic for reversing links
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReverseLinkedList(head)
// Input: Pointer to head of singly linked list
// Output: Pointer to new head (which was last node)
BEGIN
    SET prev ← NULL
    SET current ← head
    
    WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
        SET next ← current.next    // Save next node
        SET current.next ← prev     // Reverse the link
        SET prev ← current          // Move prev forward
        SET current ← next         // Move current forward
    END WHILE
    
    SET head ← prev    // Update head to new first node
    RETURN head
END
examples.explanation: This iterative algorithm reverses the linked list in-place using three pointers. The key idea is to reverse each link direction as we traverse. We save the next node before changing current.next because we would otherwise lose the reference. After the loop, prev points to the new head (original last node), and all links point backwards. Time: O(n), Space: O(1).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - visits each node exactly once
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only three pointers used regardless of list size
